title: Distributive Property
essential_question: How can you use the Distributive Property to find products?
strategies: Break one factor into two smaller addends
You can also split with subtraction: a × b = a × (c − d)

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.4 'Distributive Property' pp.151-154
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic F — Distributive Property and Properties of Multiplication
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

pretest.question: What does the Distributive Property let you do?
choices: A. Change the order of factors
B. Break apart a factor and multiply in parts
pretest.explanation: Break apart, multiply each part, then add.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.4 'Distributive Property' pp.151-154

learn.title: The Distributive Property
learn.content: You can break apart one factor into two smaller numbers, multiply each separately, then add. Example: 5 × 7 = 5 × (5 + 2) = (5 × 5) + (5 × 2) = 25 + 10 = 35.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.4 'Distributive Property' pp.151-154
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic F — Distributive Property and Properties of Multiplication
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Breaking Apart with Arrays
learn.content: Draw a 4 × 7 array. Split it into 4 × 4 and 4 × 3. Count each part: 16 + 12 = 28. The whole array has 28 dots — the same answer as 4 × 7.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.4 'Distributive Property' pp.151-154
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic F — Distributive Property and Properties of Multiplication
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Choosing How to Break Apart
learn.content: Break the bigger factor into numbers you know well. If you know your 5s facts, break 7 into 5 + 2. If you know your doubles, break 8 into 4 + 4. There's no single right way — pick easy pieces.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.4 'Distributive Property' pp.151-154
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic F — Distributive Property and Properties of Multiplication
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Break Apart with Subtraction
learn.content: You can also split a factor using subtraction. 7 × 9 is tricky, but 7 × 10 is easy. Think: 7 × 9 = 7 × (10 − 1) = 70 − 7 = 63. Multiplying by 10 and subtracting once is often faster.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.4 'Distributive Property' pp.151-154
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic F — Distributive Property and Properties of Multiplication
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Why It Works — The Area Picture
learn.content: Picture a rectangle 6 units wide and 8 units tall. Slice it horizontally: 6 × 5 on top, 6 × 3 on bottom. The two pieces together still make the full rectangle: 30 + 18 = 48. The Distributive Property is just slicing and adding the pieces.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.4 'Distributive Property' pp.151-154
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic F — Distributive Property and Properties of Multiplication
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 두 부분 모두에 곱하기 — 절대 빠뜨리지 마세요
learn.content: 분배법칙의 가장 흔한 실수: 한 부분만 곱하고 멈추기. 예: 8 × 7 = 8 × (5 + 2).   ❌ 잘못: 8 × 5 = 40 → 답 40 (8 × 2를 빠뜨림).   ✅ 올바름: 8 × 5 = 40, 8 × 2 = 16, 40 + 16 = 56. 기억법: 분배(distribute)는 '나눠주다' — 두 부분 모두에 곱셈을 나눠줘야 함. 검증: 두 작은 인자가 원래 인자가 되도록 더해야 함 (5 + 2 = 7).

try.question: Break 8 into 4 + 4 to find 6 × 8. What is 6 × (4 + 4)?
try.explanation: 6 × 4 = 24. Both parts are the same, so 24 + 24 = 48.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.4 Lesson 4.4 'Distributive Property' pp.151-154
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic F — Distributive Property and Properties of Multiplication

hints: Subtract one copy of 6 to shrink it to 6 × 9.

try.question: Which is a correct way to use the Distributive Property for 7 × 8?
try.explanation: All three splits give 56. The Distributive Property has many valid ways to break apart a factor.
hints: Check each split by computing the products.
If the split adds back to 8, it gives the correct answer.

feedback.correct: Right — every way that splits 8 correctly gives 56.
feedback.incorrect: Each choice breaks 8 into parts that add back to 8. All three give 56. Answer is D.

try.feedback_correct: Right — every way that splits 8 correctly gives 56.

practice_r2.question: Which shows 8 × 6 using the Distributive Property?
practice_r2.explanation: All three splits add back to 6, so all give 8 × 6 = 48.
hints: Check each: do the two smaller factors add up to 6?
If yes, the split is valid.

feedback.correct: Right — any valid split of 6 gives 48.
feedback.incorrect: 3+3=6, 4+2=6, 5+1=6. All three splits give 48. Answer is D.

practice_r2.feedback_correct: Right — any valid split of 6 gives 48.

hints: Do the two smaller factors add back to 9?

feedback.correct: Right — 4 + 5 = 9, and the parts add to 45.

practice_r2.question: Find the difference: 902 − 567.
practice_r2.explanation: 902−567: 2−7 borrow → 12−7=5; tens 0−6 borrow → 10−7=3 (after lending); hundreds 8−5=3. Result: 335.
hints: Borrow across the zero in tens.
